notes.note: This letter is from Cicero's brother Quintus, not from Cicero himself; 'S. D.' = salutem dicit, 'sends greetings.'

The line quoted in Greek ('cold is the bitterest enemy of a delicate skin') is attributed to the Athenian tragedian Euripides; Quintus says he treats each of Euripides's verses as a single piece of evidence.

notes.anchor: Quintus the son sends you his greetings
notes.note: 'Quintus the son' is the writer's own son, Cicero's nephew, who was travelling with the family.
